title: centaurea rigida var. schizophylla
intro: Centaurea rigida var. schizophylla, also known as the rigid centaury or mountain centaury, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Consuming Centaurea rigida var. schizophylla is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
